toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to:
1. Run the tests
2. Collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected by setting environment variables for the crash dump collection. The standard approach is:

1. Using `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` and `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` environment variables to collect crash dumps when the .NET runtime itself crashes.
2. Using `dotnet test` with the `--blame-crash` option which uses the Microsoft.TestPlatform blame data collector to collect crash dumps.


Since the project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`, the `--blame-crash` flag should work with `dotnet test`, so I'll run the tests with that option to capture any crash dumps if the test host process fails.

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-40585a2eda4e43c5a62c9cc2b7386418 && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what ran:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a crash dump (`.dmp` file) if the test host process crashes unexpectedly. Since no crashes occurred this time, no dump was generated.

**Results:** 3/3 tests passed, build succeeded in ~20s.
